title: Detect and flag unusual withdrawal patterns
description: The system shall detect rapid consecutive withdrawals (more than 3 within 5 minutes) and amounts significantly outside the user's historical average, and flag the account for review.
rationale: To identify potential fraud or errors quickly to prevent financial loss and maintain customer trust.
business_value: Early detection of fraudulent activities can help in preventing financial losses and maintaining the integrity of the banking system.

source.description: As a system, I need detect unusual withdrawal patterns (e.g., rapid consecutive withdrawals, unusual amounts) and flag the account for review so that potential fraud or errors are identified quickly.

acceptance_criteria: Given the system is monitoring withdrawal transactions, When a user performs rapid consecutive withdrawals (e.g., 4 withdrawals within 5 minutes), Then the system flags the account for review.
Given the system is monitoring withdrawal transactions, When a user performs a withdrawal of an unusual amount (e.g., 10x the average), Then the system flags the account for review.
preconditions: The system must be operational and actively monitoring withdrawal transactions.
postconditions: The system must flag the account for review if it detects rapid consecutive withdrawals or an unusual amount.
functional_constraints: The detection algorithm must operate within a response time of less than 2 minutes to ensure timely flagging.
security_requirements: All withdrawal transactions must be securely logged and accessible only by authorized personnel.
User data must be encrypted at rest and in transit.

non_functional_requirements.performance: The system must handle up to 1000 concurrent users without significant performance degradation.
non_functional_requirements.availability: The system must be available 99.9% of the time with minimal downtime.
non_functional_requirements.scalability: The system must scale horizontally to accommodate growth in user base and transaction volume.
non_functional_requirements.usability: Error messages must be clear and actionable, and interfaces should be intuitive.

edge_cases: What happens if a user makes withdrawals during a holiday weekend when historical average transactions are lower?
How the system behaves if there is a sudden spike in transaction volume, leading to potential delays in detecting patterns?
dependencies: Historical transaction data for each user
Real-time monitoring capabilities of the system

interfaces.name: User Transaction Database
interfaces.description: Used to store and access historical transaction data for users.

interfaces.name: Withdrawal API
interfaces.description: Interface through which withdrawal transactions are submitted for monitoring.

test_cases.description: User makes 4 withdrawals within a span of 5 minutes.
test_cases.expected_result: The system flags the account for review.

test_cases.description: User makes a withdrawal amount that is 10x their historical average.
test_cases.expected_result: The system flags the account for review.
